title: [main.c:72-79] lora_rx_handler data parameter unused but passed to callback
description:
Gap: The standalone LoRa RX handler receives data but does nothing with it beyond logging the length.

What: Lines 72-79 define lora_rx_handler which marks both user_data AND data as ARG_UNUSED, but only logs the length. While the comment says 'RX processing not implemented', the data pointer is received and could be used for timing attacks if an attacker can influence when frames arrive.

Why it matters: This is a ponytail comment acknowledging incomplete implementation, but the function signature implies data processing occurs. An auditor might miss that no actual processing happens. Either implement minimal validation or add a more prominent comment that this is a no-op stub.
close_reason: Intentional stub: standalone mode handler is explicitly marked as unimplemented with ponytail comment (line 78). The ARG_UNUSED annotations and 'RX processing not implemented - add when needed' comment clearly document this is a no-op for testing. Standalone mode is only enabled when CONFIG_LICHEN_LORA_L2 is set WITHOUT CONFIG_LICHEN_L2. No change needed.
